Definition (EN): An interface definition is a kind of connection definition (see 7.13 ) whose ends are restricted to be port definitions (see 7.12 ). An interface usage is a kind of connection that is usage of an interface definition. The ends of an interface usage are restricted to be port usages.
Context/Example (EN): An interface is simply a connection all of whose ends are ports.

Equivalent (JP): インターフェース定義
Definition (EN): An interface definition is a kind of connection definition (see 7.13 ) whose ends are restricted to be port definitions (see 7.12 ).
Context/Example (EN): An interface definition is a kind of connection definition (see 7.13 ) whose ends are restricted to be port definitions (see 7.12 ).

Equivalent (JP): インターフェース使用
Definition (EN): An interface usage is a kind of connection that is usage of an interface definition. The ends of an interface usage are restricted to be port usages.
Context/Example (EN): An interface usage is a kind of connection that is usage of an interface definition.

Definition (EN): A port definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a connection point to enable interactions between occurrences (most commonly parts).
Context/Example (EN): An interface definition is a kind of connection definition (see 7.13 ) whose ends are restricted to be port definitions (see 7.12 ).

Definition (EN): A port usage is a kind of occurrence usage definition that is a usage of a port definition.
Context/Example (EN): The ends of an interface usage are restricted to be port usages.

Definition (EN): Each port definition has a conjugated port definition whose directed features are conjugate to those of the original port definition.
Context/Example (EN): Each port definition has a conjugated port definition whose directed features are conjugate to those of the original port definition.

Definition (EN): Flows of values and items between parts and actions (see 7.16 ).
Context/Example (EN): When modeling physical interactions, an interface definition or usage can contain constraints (see 7.20 ) to constrain the values of the features of the ports on its ends.

Definition (EN): Messages are typically used to model abstract logical interaction between part usages in a certain context, which may be realized in a more detailed model using streaming or succession flows (or transfers from send actions to accept actions, see 7.17 ).
Context/Example (EN): If a send action (see 7.17.7 ) is used to send a transfer via a port that is at one end of a binary interface, then the port at the other end of the interface automatically becomes the target of the transfer.

Definition (EN): Features of the ports on its ends that are constrained by conservation laws across the interface (e.g., Kirchhoff’s Laws).
Context/Example (EN): When specifying an interface between electrical components, the across and through variables are port features defined as Voltage and Current quantities, respectively.

Definition (EN): Features of the ports on its ends that are constrained by conservation laws across the interface (e.g., Kirchhoff’s Laws).
Context/Example (EN): When specifying an interface between electrical components, the across and through variables are port features defined as Voltage and Current quantities, respectively.

Equivalent (JP): バイナリインターフェース
Definition (EN): A binary interface definition or usage is an interface that has exactly two ends.
Context/Example (EN): For a binary interface definition or usage, the base definition and usage are further restricted to BinaryInterface and binaryInterfaces, which enforce that the interface has exactly two ends.

Definition (EN): A quantity representing electrical potential difference.
Context/Example (EN): When specifying an interface between electrical components, the across and through variables are port features defined as Voltage and Current quantities, respectively.

Definition (EN): A quantity representing the rate of flow of electric charge.
Context/Example (EN): When specifying an interface between electrical components, the across and through variables are port features defined as Voltage and Current quantities, respectively.
